manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser, belt.
Your opponent's team words are: horseshoe, bridge, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Distractor words are: cell, telescope, cycle, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
The kill words are: .

message.reasoning:

We have our team words: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser (presumably "laser"), belt.

Goal: pick a clue linking multiple of them. Single-word clue.

We must be mindful not to accidentally connect to opponent's words or distractors, otherwise partner might guess them. But the clue can be ambiguous but we try to be safe.

What's a concept that relates to e.g., "band", "pipe", "laser", "press"? Could be "metal"? Fish not metal. Could use "sound" - band (music), pipe (sound), press (press = news?), maybe not.

Maybe "steam": steam pipe, steam press (like a steam press iron), steam belt? No.

"Red": red fish? red spot? red belt (martial arts). Red band? red press? Not great.

"Game": maybe "football"? belt (title belt), band (band, like ring?), pipe (pipe?), spot (spot the ball), press (press?).

"Trap": maybe not.

"Cold": cold fish, cold press (oil?), cold belt? Cold spot, cold pipe, cold laser? Possibly.

"Laser" is a technology; "beam" could relate to laser, pipe (beam?), band (beam? no). Could be "light": laser is light, light pipe (optical fiber), light press? Light band? Light fish? Light belt? Light spot (spotlight). Light press? Light game? Light bed? Not good.

Maybe "spot" could be a "dot". "dot" could relate to "laser" (dot?), "pipe" (dot?), not.

"Belt" also can be "belt". Could clue "martial". Martial arts belt, martial band? NotPerhaps "press" could be "iron". Iron press, iron belt (like iron belt?), iron pipe, iron band (metal ring), iron fish (maybe a toy), iron spot? Iron laser? Hmm.

Could use "iron". It's a metal used for pipe, press (ironing), belt (iron belt?), band (iron band = chain). Laser iron? Not.

Maybe "sauce"? fish sauce, belt? No.

"Sleep": bed, fish (sleep?), spot (sleeping?), not.

Maybe we choose clue "electric". Electric fish (electric eel), electric belt (electric belt?), electric pipe (electric pipe?), electric band (electric band - a guitar band). Electric press? Not.

Better: "tackle" - fish tackle, tackle box (not others). Not.

What about "under" - under the sea (fish), under the bed, under the spot? No.

Maybe clue "record": band (record), press (press release), game (record?), pipe (record? no). Not.

Maybe we pick just 2-3 words with a safe clue.

For example: clue "sleep" relates to bed, spot (sleeping spot?), fish (sleep? fish sleep). Belt? Not.

Better: clue "stream". Stream fish, stream bed (river bed), stream press? Not.

Clue "belt" maybe not.

Could use "belt" to link band (belt band), pipe (belt pipe?), not.

Perhaps focus on "press", "band", "game", "laser" - all could be "arcade"? Press (press start), game (arcade game), laser (laser tag), band (arcade band? no).

Maybe "laser" and "press" and "band" and "pipe" all are "metal". Metal band, metal pipe, metal press (metal press), metal laser (metal cutting laser). That's 4 words: band pipe, press, laser. Also "metal fish"? Not. We can target those four.

Clue: "metal". Must check no opponent/distractor words include metal. Opponents: horseshoe (metal?), bridge (metal?), not. Distractors include "cell", "telescope", "cycle", "theater", "organ", "wave", "cross", "night". None are metal but "organ" could be metal organ instrument. But risk.

But clue "metal" may lead partner to guess "metal fish"? There is "metal fish" maybe a novelty but less common. They might be cautious.

However we must ensure "metal" isn't part of any words (like "metal" not a word in lists). It's fine.

Thus we output:

CLUE: metal
TARGETS: band, pipe, press, laser

That's short.

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn.
